sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is used concurrently with radiation for locally advanced disease, as adjuvant therapy, and as primary treatment for recurrent/metastatic disease. The landscape includes traditional cytotoxic chemotherapy, anti-angiogenic agents (bevacizumab), immune checkpoint inhibitors (pembrolizumab, cemiplimab, atezolizumab, nivolumab), and antibody-drug conjugates (tisotumab vedotin). Treatment selection is increasingly guided by biomarkers (PD-L1, MSI-H/dMMR, TMB-H, HER2, NTRK, RET).

cards.name: Cisplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab ± Bevacizumab
cards.detail:
Setting: First-line for recurrent/metastatic, PD-L1-positive (CPS ≥1) cervical cancer.
Drugs:
- Cisplatin 50 mg/m2 IV Day 1
- Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV Day 1
- Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Day 1
- Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV Day 1 (Optional)
Cycle: Every 21 days
Key toxicities: Neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia, fatigue, alopecia, neuropathy, nausea, hypertension, proteinuria, immune-mediated adverse reactions.

cards.name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab
cards.detail:
Setting: First-line for recurrent/metastatic cervical cancer.
Drugs:
- Carboplatin AUC 5-6 IV Day 1
- Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV Day 1
- Atezolizumab 840 mg IV Day 1
- Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV Day 1
Cycle: Every 21 days
Key toxicities: Neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia, fatigue, alopecia, neuropathy, nausea, hypertension, proteinuria, immune-mediated adverse reactions.

timeline.title: Concurrent Chemoradiation (Locally Advanced)
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Cisplatin ± Pembrolizumab (Category 1 for FIGO 2014 stage IIIA, IIIB, IVA; Category 2B for select FIGO 2018 stage III-IVA). Carboplatin ± Pembrolizumab if cisplatin intolerant. Rationale: Category 1 evidence from randomized trials showing improved survival with concurrent cisplatin over RT alone. KEYNOTE-A18 adds pembrolizumab for improved PFS/OS in stage III-IVA. Alternatives: If single-agent cisplatin/carboplatin unavailable: Capecitabine/Mitomycin, Gemcitabine, Paclitaxel. Induction Carboplatin/Paclitaxel followed by chemoradiation (INTERLACE protocol) can be considered. Biomarker requirements: PD-L1 testing recommended for pembrolizumab eligibility.

timeline.title: Adjuvant (High-Risk)
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: Pelvic EBRT + concurrent cisplatin (or carboplatin if cisplatin intolerant) ± brachytherapy. Rationale: Category 1 evidence (GOG 109) for improved survival with concurrent chemoradiation in high-risk disease (positive nodes/margins/parametrium). Alternatives: Capecitabine/Mitomycin, Gemcitabine, Paclitaxel as radiosensitizers if platinum unavailable. Biomarker requirements: None.

timeline.title: First-Line Recurrent/Metastatic
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: For PD-L1-positive tumors (CPS ≥1): Cisplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab ± Bevacizumab (Category 1) OR Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Pembrolizumab ± Bevacizumab (Category 1). Cisplatin/Paclitaxel + Bevacizumab (Category 1) OR Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Bevacizumab. Cisplatin/Paclitaxel + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab (Category 1) OR Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab (Category 1). Rationale: KEYNOTE-826 (pembrolizumab) and BEATcc (atezolizumab) trials demonstrated significant OS/PFS benefits with addition of IO to chemo ± bevacizumab in first-line setting. Alternatives: Cisplatin/Paclitaxel (Category 1), Carboplatin/Paclitaxel (Category 1; Category 1 for prior cisplatin), Paclitaxel/Topotecan + Bevacizumab (Category 1). Biomarker requirements: PD-L1 (CPS ≥1) required for pembrolizumab regimens.

timeline.title: Second-Line/Subsequent
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: TMB-H tumors: Pembrolizumab. PD-L1-positive: Pembrolizumab. MSI-H/dMMR tumors: Pembrolizumab. Tisotumab vedotin-tftv (Category 1). Rationale: Pembrolizumab approved for TMB-H, PD-L1+, MSI-H/dMMR tumors. Tisotumab vedotin showed OS benefit over chemotherapy in innovaTV-301 trial. Alternatives: Bevacizumab, Paclitaxel, Albumin-bound Paclitaxel, Docetaxel, Fluorouracil, Gemcitabine, Pemetrexed, Topotecan, Vinorelbine, Irinotecan, Cemiplimab, Ipilimumab + Nivolumab. Biomarker requirements: TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb), PD-L1, MSI-H/dMMR by FDA-approved assay.

timeline.title: Biomarker-Directed
timeline.body: Preferred regimen: HER2-positive (IHC 3+ or 2+): Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki. HER2-mutant: Neratinib. RET gene fusion-positive: Selpercatinib. NTRK gene fusion-positive: Larotrectinib, Entrectinib, Repotrectinib. PD-L1-positive: Nivolumab; Pembrolizumab + Tisotumab vedotin-tftv. Rationale: Tumor-agnostic approvals based on high response rates in basket trials (DESTINY-PanTumor02, SUMMIT, LIBRETTO-001, STARTRK, TRIDENT-1, CheckMate-358, innovaTV 205). Alternatives: None for specific biomarkers. Biomarker requirements: HER2, RET, NTRK, PD-L1 testing by validated assay.
